data.output:
638	// TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness verifies that two runs on the same bead-id
639	// produce distinct preserve refs (concurrent hidden-ref uniqueness).
640	// Exercises real git plumbing and the script harness producing actual
641	// conflicting commits — no fakes for the components under test.
642	func TestExecuteBeadHiddenRefUniqueness(t *testing.T) {
643		workDir := t.TempDir()
644	
645		scrubEnv := func() []string {
646			parent := os.Environ()
647			env := make([]string, 0, len(parent))
648			for _, kv := range parent {
649				if strings.HasPrefix(kv, "GIT_") {
650					continue
651				}
652				env = append(env, kv)
653			}
654			return env
655		}
656		runGit := func(args ...string) string {
657			t.Helper()
658			cmd := exec.Command("git", args...)
659			cmd.Dir = workDir
660			cmd.Env = scrubEnv()
661			out, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
662			require.NoError(t, err, "git %v: %s", args, string(out))
663			return strings.TrimSpace(string(out))
664		}
665	
666		runGit("init", "-b", "main")
667		runGit("config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
668		runGit("config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
669		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0o644))
670		runGit("add", ".")
671		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
672	
673		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
674			ID:        "shared-bead",
675			Title:     "Test execute-bead hidden-ref uniqueness",
676			Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
677			IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
678		})
679		runGit("add", ".ddx/beads.jsonl")
680		runGit("commit", "-m", "chore: seed bead")
681		baseSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
682	
683		// Sibling commit conflicts with the worker's hello.txt content, forcing
684		// every run to take the merge-conflict preserve path.
685		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(workDir, "hello.txt"), []byte("sibling-content\n"), 0o644))
686		runGit("add", "hello.txt")
687		runGit("commit", "-m", "feat: sibling conflicting change")
688		siblingSHA := runGit("rev-parse", "HEAD")
689	
690		// Each run drives the script harness to emit a real commit touching the
691		// same file as the sibling, so Land() must preserve under a hidden ref.
692		directivePath := filepath.Join(t.TempDir(), "directives.txt")
693		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(directivePath, []byte(
694			"create-file hello.txt worker-content\n"+
695				"commit feat: worker add hello\n",
696		), 0o644))
697	
698		makeRun := func() agent.ExecuteBeadResult {
699			t.Helper()
700			f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
701			f.AgentRunnerOverride = agent.NewRunner(agent.Config{})
702			root := f.NewRootCommand()
703			out, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-bead", "shared-bead", "--json",
704				"--from", baseSHA,
705				"--harness", "script", "--model", directivePath)
706			require.NoError(t, err, "execute-bead should not return an error; output: %s", out)
707			return parseExecuteBeadJSON(t, out)
708		}
709	
710		res1 := makeRun()
711		res2 := makeRun()
712	
713		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res1.Outcome)
714		assert.Equal(t, "preserved", res2.Outcome)
715		assert.NotEqual(t, res1.PreserveRef, res2.PreserveRef,
716			"concurrent runs must produce distinct preserve refs")
717		// Real Land() records the ref as refs/ddx/iterations/<bead>/<attempt>-<currentTip[:12]>.
718		// The first run's currentTip is siblingSHA; the second run's tip differs
719		// because execute-bead checkpoints tracker/worktree state between runs.
720		require.Regexp(t,
721			fmt.Sprintf(`^refs/ddx/iterations/shared-bead/.+-%s$`, regexp.QuoteMeta(siblingSHA[:12])),
722			res1.PreserveRef)
723		require.Regexp(t, `^refs/ddx/iterations/shared-bead/.+-[0-9a-f]{12}$`, res2.PreserveRef)
724	
725		// Both hidden refs must exist in the real repo and resolve to their
726		// respective worker commits.
727		assert.Equal(t, res1.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", res1.PreserveRef))
728		assert.Equal(t, res2.ResultRev, runGit("rev-parse", res2.PreserveRef))
729	}
730	
731	// TestExecuteBeadNoChanges verifies that when the agent makes no commits the
732	// outcome is "no-changes".
733	func TestExecuteBeadNoChanges(t *testing.T) {
734		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
735			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
736			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // same as base — no commits made
737			wtDirty:     false,
738		}
739		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
740		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
741	
742		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
743	
744		assert.Equal(t, "no-changes", res.Outcome)
745		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, res.Status)
746		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
747		assert.Empty(t, res.PreserveRef)
748	}
749	
750	// TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits verifies that tracked file edits
751	// left uncommitted by the agent are synthesized into a commit and treated as
752	// real output rather than being discarded as "no-changes".
753	func TestExecuteBeadDirtyWorktreeWithoutCommits(t *testing.T) {
754		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
755			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
756			wtHeadRev:   "aaaa1111", // agent made no commits
757			wtDirty:     true,       // but left tracked file edits
758			synthRev:    "cccc3333", // SynthesizeCommit produces this rev
759		}
760		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
761		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
762	
763		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
764	
765		assert.NotEqual(t, "no-changes", res.Outcome, "dirty worktree should not be classified as no-changes")
766		assert.Equal(t, "cccc3333", res.ResultRev)
767		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
768		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
769		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
770		assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls)
771		assert.Equal(t, "cccc3333", git.mergeRev)
772	}
773	
774	func TestExecuteBeadMergePreservesContext(t *testing.T) {
775		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
776			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
777			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
778			mergeErr:    nil,
779		}
780		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0}}
781		f := newExecuteBeadFactory(t, git, runner)
782	
783		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
784	
785		assert.Equal(t, "merged", res.Outcome)
786		assert.Equal(t, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, res.Status)
787		assert.Equal(t, "aaaa1111", res.BaseRev)
788		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", res.ResultRev)
789		assert.Equal(t, 1, git.mergeCalls)
790		assert.Equal(t, "bbbb2222", git.mergeRev)
791	}
792	
793	func TestExecuteBeadSynthesizesPromptAndArtifacts(t *testing.T) {
794		workDir := t.TempDir()
795		seedExecuteBead(t, workDir, &bead.Bead{
796			ID:          "my-bead",
797			Title:       "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis",
798			Status:      bead.StatusOpen,
799			Priority:    0,
800			IssueType:   bead.DefaultType,
801			Description: "Replace the bare fallback prompt with deterministic bead context.",
802			Acceptance:  "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.",
803			Labels:      []string{"area:agent", "phase:build"},
804			Extra:       map[string]any{"spec-id": "FEAT-006"},
805		})
806		specPath := filepath.Join(workDir, "docs", "feature.md")
807		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(specPath), 0o755))
808		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(specPath, []byte(`---
809	ddx:
810	  id: FEAT-006
811	---
812	# Agent Service
813	`), 0o644))
814	
815		git := &fakeExecuteBeadGit{
816			mainHeadRev: "aaaa1111",
817			wtHeadRev:   "bbbb2222",
818		}
819		runner := &fakeAgentRunner{result: &agent.Result{ExitCode: 0, Harness: "mock"}}
820		f := NewCommandFactory(workDir)
821		seedDefaultExecuteBeads(t, workDir)
822		f.AgentRunnerOverride = runner
823		f.executeBeadGitOverride = git
824		f.executeBeadOrchestratorGitOverride = git
825		f.executeBeadLandingAdvancerOverride = fakeLandingAdvancerFromGit(git)
826	
827		res := runExecuteBead(t, f, git, "my-bead")
828	
829		require.NotEmpty(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
830		require.FileExists(t, runner.last.PromptFile)
831		promptRaw, err := os.ReadFile(runner.last.PromptFile)
832		require.NoError(t, err)
833		promptText := string(promptRaw)
834		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Improve execute-bead prompt synthesis")
835		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Replace the bare fallback prompt")
836		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "Prompt contains bead context and governing references.")
837		assert.Contains(t, promptText, "docs/feature.md")

2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"encoding/json"

20		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
21	)
22	
23	// fakeExecuteBeadGit is a mock implementation of executeBeadGitOps for tests.
24	type fakeExecuteBeadGit struct {
25		mu sync.Mutex
26	
27		// mainHeadRev is returned by HeadRev/ResolveRev for the main working dir.
28		mainHeadRev string
29		// headRevSeq, when set, is returned in order for successive main-dir HeadRev calls.
30		headRevSeq []string
31		headRevIdx int
32		// wtHeadRev is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths (after agent run).
33		wtHeadRev string
34		// wtDirty is returned by IsDirty for worktree paths.
35		wtDirty bool
36		// synthRev, if set, is applied as wtHeadRev when SynthesizeCommit is called.
37		synthRev string
38		// wtHeadRevErr, if set, is returned by HeadRev for worktree paths.
39		wtHeadRevErr error
40		dirty        bool
41		mergeErr     error
42		updateRefErr error
43	
44		addedWTs   []string
45		addedWTRev string
46		removedWTs []string
47		refs       map[string]string // ref -> sha recorded by UpdateRef
48		worktrees  []string          // paths returned by WorktreeList
49	
50		mergeCalls int
